100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 18, 1926, Britain faced a general strike as workers rallied for better wages and working conditions, bringing major industries to a standstill. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate continued debates on immigration reform, with growing tensions over the influx of foreign workers. In the realm of international relations, tensions rose between France and Germany as reparations discussions remained unresolved following World War I.
Updated: Monday, May 18, 2026 at 09:46:33 AM CDT
